raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.0,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.5":"The draft contains severe intra-paragraph repetition (entire propositions restated 2–3 times per paragraph) whose elimination likely requires merging or reordering passages, not just sentence-level edits; structural reorganization is therefore central but not yet confirmed load-bearing pending clarification.","R1.6":"Writing polish is the dominant task: the opening paragraph restates the 'combining vetting signals from multiple independent classifiers' clause three times verbatim, the BLS/Mandel-Agol paragraph re-explains already-cited methods in redundant apposition, and the results paragraph doubles back on its own summary sentence; eliminating this pervasive redundancy and tightening prose is the clearest load-bearing obligation of 'improve this draft.'"},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the improvement be confined to sentence-level writing quality—trimming repetition, tightening prose, removing redundant appositions—while preserving the current four-paragraph structure, or do you also authorize restructuring the section (e.g., merging paragraphs, reordering content, or expanding thin passages such as the consensus-aggregation rule)?"}

rationale.R1.5: The draft contains severe intra-paragraph repetition (entire propositions restated 2–3 times per paragraph) whose elimination likely requires merging or reordering passages, not just sentence-level edits; structural reorganization is therefore central but not yet confirmed load-bearing pending clarification.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is the dominant task: the opening paragraph restates the 'combining vetting signals from multiple independent classifiers' clause three times verbatim, the BLS/Mandel-Agol paragraph re-explains already-cited methods in redundant apposition, and the results paragraph doubles back on its own summary sentence; eliminating this pervasive redundancy and tightening prose is the clearest load-bearing obligation of 'improve this draft.'

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement be confined to sentence-level writing quality—trimming repetition, tightening prose, removing redundant appositions—while preserving the current four-paragraph structure, or do you also authorize restructuring the section (e.g., merging paragraphs, reordering content, or expanding thin passages such as the consensus-aggregation rule)?
